description: Xilinx ZCU102 (Zynq UltraScale+ MPSoC, XCZU9EG) with ADI MxFE FMC daughter cards. Profiles in this catalog cover validated MxFE operating points; profile data (manifests + patches + upstream pristine subtrees) lives in each consuming project under its own `socks.json::adi.no_os_subtree` and `socks.json::adi.hdl_subtree`. The catalog is name-only — it lists which profiles are known + their high-level operating points + their hardware-validation status, NOT the patch bytes.
fmc_daughter_cards_supported: ad9081-fmca-ebz (reference design; some profiles work on AD9986-FMCA via chip-ID acceptance patch)

6144-jesd204b-m8l4.description: 61.44 MHz PL link clock, 2.4576 Gbps lane rate, JESD204B 8B/10B encoding, M=8 / L=4 / S=1 / NP=16 (RX = 4 ADC RF inputs each emitted as I/Q). HMC7044 PLL2 at 2.94912 GHz from a 122.88 MHz VCXO; SYSREF at 1.92 MHz LMFC.
6144-jesd204b-m8l4.validation_summary: Manual hardware re-validation PASS on real ZCU102 + AD9986-FMCA hardware. The consuming project reached all five UART pass markers for the 6144-jesd204b-m8l4 profile.

sources_when_picking_a_new_profile.purpose: ADI no-OS reference firmware for AD9081/AD9986; vendored as pristine + patch series in consuming projects.
ADI HDL reference designs (ad9081_fmca_ebz, etc.); vendored as pristine + per-profile patch series in consuming projects.
Cross-check authoritative source for M/L/S/NP combos and crossbar / converter-select values per profile.
Enumeration of FDDC virtual converter IDs (FDDC0_I = 0, FDDC0_Q = 1, FDDC1_I = 2, FDDC1_Q = 3, FDDC2_I = 4, ..., FDDC5_Q = 11).
Default routing of ADC pairs to FDDC virtual converters (ADC0 → FDDC0/2, ADC1 → FDDC1/3, ADC2 → FDDC4/6, ADC3 → FDDC5/7).

Per-profile array selecting which FDDCs are emitted on which JESD virtual converters. 8-element array maps VC[0..7] -> FDDC enum values; cross-reference with the API enum to determine VC-to-physical-ADC mapping.

TX-side equivalents (AD9081_TX_JESD_M/L/S/NP, AD9081_TX_LOGICAL_LANE_MAPPING, AD9081_TX_DAC_CHAN_CROSSBAR, AD9081_TX_DAC_MAIN_CHAN_CROSSBAR). The pinned no-OS app at b45866d does NOT have a TX_LINK_CONVERTER_SELECT analog to RX; TX physical mapping requires a TX-data-path validation rather than inference from link-up markers alone.
Per-profile HMC7044 PLL2 + output-divider + SYSREF settings for the device + FPGA refclks.

methodology_for_new_profile_authoring.summary: Three-layer derivation: (1) read the no-OS app's per-profile *_LINK_CONVERTER_SELECT array; (2) cross-reference with the AD9081 API enum (adi_ad9081_adc_fine_ddc_converter_e for RX, equivalent DAC enum for TX); (3) cross-reference with the API's default crossbar datapath. (4) Cross-check the result against ADI Linux DTS overlay for the same M/L/S/NP combo.
methodology_for_new_profile_authoring.rx_status: Working RX methodology validated for the 6144-jesd204b-m8l4 profile (FDDC0/1/4/5 → ADC0/1/2/3).
methodology_for_new_profile_authoring.tx_status: TX methodology only partially proven from no-OS source. The pinned no-OS app has no TX LINK_CONVERTER_SELECT analog to RX; TX VC-to-DAC physical mapping requires a TX-data-path validation step (e.g., DAC output spectrum capture + per-channel correlation) before claiming a definitive mapping.
methodology_for_new_profile_authoring.deferred_field_shape: When the methodology is run-able but the result is unproven, populate the manifest's operating_point.<rx|tx>.physical_mapping with status='methodology-only' + source_files[] + crossbar_note + observed values, and explicitly disclaim VC-to-physical mapping. Do NOT leave the field empty / null / fabricated.

catalog_authoring_rules.genericization: This catalog file is in the SOCKS skill repo (~/.claude/skills/socks/references/boards/zcu102/) and is shared by every consuming project on this board. Do NOT include project-specific paths, workspace-rooted paths, validated_evidence fields, first_validated_thread fields, or any other consuming-project provenance. Provenance lives in each consuming project's findings docs and links upward to a catalog entry by name only.
catalog_authoring_rules.validated_date_lifecycle: Set validated_date to a real ISO date only after a real hardware run on real ZCU102 + AD9986-FMCA hardware reaches all five UART pass markers. Software-only checks (patches apply, JSON parses, fingerprint scan clean) are NOT sufficient to set validated_date — they prove the patch system is structurally correct, not that the resulting configuration trains a JESD link in vivo.
catalog_authoring_rules.profile_addition: New profiles authored in any consuming project are added here by name + operating_point summary + uart_pass_markers + patch_layout_contract. The catalog does NOT carry the patch bytes themselves — those live in each consuming project's own ADI subtree per its socks.json::adi configuration.
